problem: How are long-term memories physically encoded in neural tissue?
mechanism: Vagus nerve stimulation modulating brain-gut axis
hypothesis: How are long-term memories physically encoded in neural tissue? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Vagus. Vagus nerve stimulation modulating brain-gut axis directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: EEG/MEG signal analysis with 15 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
